source: App portfolio approach: $185K/mo proof from multiple apps
title: Developer grew app portfolio to $185K/mo. Don't bet on one app. Build 5-10 apps, kill losers, 2x down on winners. Portfolio diversification reduces risk.
description: Build multiple apps in parallel. Launch all. Track 60 days. Kill bottom 50%. Reinvest into winners. Diversification proven at scale.
actionable_steps: 1. Build 5-10 micro apps in parallel 2. Launch all with minimal marketing 3. Track metrics for 60 days 4. Kill bottom 50% performers 5. Reinvest ad spend into top performers 6. Repeat quarterly
reviewer_notes: Case study Feb 2026. $185K/mo verified. Portfolio approach vs single app bet.

source: Build for distribution first not features - 2026 pivot
title: Distribution > perfection. Most devs build for months then wonder why nobody shows. Flip it: validate distribution BEFORE building. Test demand first.
description: Validate distribution channel before building full app. Waitlist + ads test demand. Only build if 100+ signups in 2 weeks. Launch with distribution ready.
actionable_steps: 1. Pick distribution channel first (TikTok/Reddit/ProductHunt) 2. Create waitlist landing 3. Run ads/content to test demand 4. Only build if 100+ signups in 2 weeks 5. Launch with distribution ready
reviewer_notes: Strategic shift Feb 2026. Distribution-first validated. Demand before build.

source: Indie dev reality: 8 apps shipped = $1464 revenue in 2025
title: Real numbers: 8 apps in 2025 made $1,464 total after Apple fees. Biggest lesson: marketing and distribution were weakness. Code is not bottleneck.
description: Marketing > coding for indie apps. Budget 50% time on distribution. Launch faster with worse code. Track cost per install from day 1.
actionable_steps: 1. Accept marketing > coding 2. Budget 50% time on distribution 3. Launch faster with worse code 4. Test marketing before features 5. Track cost per install day 1
reviewer_notes: Reality check Feb 2026. $1,464 verified. Marketing bottleneck not code.

source: Tight MVP focus = biggest app cost reduction tactic
title: Ruthless MVP focus reduces costs more than anything. 1 core problem, 1 main user, 1 primary action. Budget: 50% MVP, 30% marketing, 20% buffer. Launch in 4 weeks max.
description: Limit scope radically. Single problem, single user, single action. Tight MVP speeds launch and improves clarity. 30% budget to marketing non-negotiable.
actionable_steps: 1. Define single core problem 2. Pick ONE user persona 3. Build ONE primary action 4. Cut everything else 5. Allocate 30% budget to marketing 6. Launch in 4 weeks max
reviewer_notes: Cost reduction Feb 2026. MVP scoping = biggest lever. 30% marketing budget key.

title: Cross-platform frameworks cut dev time 60% vs native. Single codebase for iOS + Android. For indie devs this is non-negotiable in 2026. Share 80%+ code.
description: React Native or Flutter mandatory for indie speed. Expo for rapid setup. Share 80%+ code between platforms. Only go native for hardware features.
actionable_steps: 1. Choose React Native (community) or Flutter (performance) 2. Use Expo for RN rapid setup 3. Share 80%+ code between platforms 4. Only native for specific hardware
reviewer_notes: Speed advantage Feb 2026. 60% faster verified. Cross-platform non-negotiable.

source: 2026 app challenge = mastering niche not code
title: Tech trends mean beginners can build apps. Real challenge: mastering the niche. Pick underserved micro-niche (10K-100K TAM), dominate that, then expand. Riches in niches.
description: Niche mastery > coding skill. Research micro-niche pain. Build MVP solving only that. Dominate niche search. Expand after product-market fit.
actionable_steps: 1. Pick micro-niche 10K-100K TAM 2. Research #1 pain (Reddit/forums) 3. Build MVP solving ONLY that pain 4. Dominate search for specific problem 5. Expand after PMF
reviewer_notes: Strategic Feb 2026. Niche-first proven. Code commoditized, niche knowledge scarce.

source: AI + low-code = individual founders building SaaS solo
title: Individual founders build specialized SaaS with AI platforms. No dev teams needed. AI and low-code reduce first version costs 80%. Speed to market is moat.
description: Use AI platforms (Cursor, v0.dev, Lovable) for prototyping. Generate 70% code with AI. Focus human time on business logic. Launch v1 in 2 weeks.
actionable_steps: 1. Use AI platforms (Cursor/v0/Lovable) for prototyping 2. Generate 70% code with AI 3. Focus human time on business logic 4. Launch v1 in 2 weeks 5. Iterate on feedback
reviewer_notes: Tech shift Feb 2026. AI enables solo SaaS. 80% cost reduction. Speed moat.

source: App speed = competitive advantage, build in weeks not months
title: Speed captures opportunities before competitors react. Build and validate in weeks not months. First mover advantage real in micro-niches. 4-week hard deadline for MVP.
description: Set 4-week MVP deadline. Cut scope ruthlessly. Launch with waitlist from week 1. Validate demand before features. Iterate weekly on metrics.
actionable_steps: 1. Set 4-week hard deadline for MVP 2. Cut scope ruthlessly to hit deadline 3. Launch with waitlist from week 1 4. Validate demand before features 5. Iterate weekly based on metrics
reviewer_notes: Speed advantage Feb 2026. Weeks not months. First mover advantage in micro-niches.
